name: sentrypeer
title: sentrypeer

commands.name: sentrypeer
commands.description: A distributed list of bad IP addresses and phone numbers collected via a SIP Honeypot

arguments.description: Set 'sentrypeer.db' location or use SENTRYPEER_DB_FILE env
arguments.raw: -f <DB_FILE>                 Set 'sentrypeer.db' location or use SENTRYPEER_DB_FILE env

arguments.description: Enable json logging or use SENTRYPEER_JSON_LOG env
arguments.raw: -j                           Enable json logging or use SENTRYPEER_JSON_LOG env

arguments.description: Enable Peer to Peer mode or use SENTRYPEER_PEER_TO_PEER env
arguments.raw: -p                           Enable Peer to Peer mode or use SENTRYPEER_PEER_TO_PEER env

arguments.description: Set Peer to Peer bootstrap node or use SENTRYPEER_BOOTSTRAP_NODE env
arguments.raw: -b <BOOTSTRAP_NODE>          Set Peer to Peer bootstrap node or use SENTRYPEER_BOOTSTRAP_NODE env

arguments.description: Set OAuth 2 client ID or use SENTRYPEER_OAUTH2_CLIENT_ID env to get a Bearer token for WebHook
arguments.raw: -i <CLIENT_ID>               Set OAuth 2 client ID or use SENTRYPEER_OAUTH2_CLIENT_ID env to get a Bearer token for WebHook

arguments.description: Set OAuth 2 client secret or use SENTRYPEER_OAUTH2_CLIENT_SECRET env to get a Bearer token for WebHook
arguments.raw: -c <CLIENT_SECRET>           Set OAuth 2 client secret or use SENTRYPEER_OAUTH2_CLIENT_SECRET env to get a Bearer token for WebHook

arguments.description: Enable RESTful API mode or use SENTRYPEER_API env
arguments.raw: -a                           Enable RESTful API mode or use SENTRYPEER_API env

arguments.description: Set WebHook URL for bad actor json POSTs or use SENTRYPEER_WEBHOOK_URL env
arguments.raw: -w <WEBHOOK_URL>             Set WebHook URL for bad actor json POSTs or use SENTRYPEER_WEBHOOK_URL env

arguments.description: Enable SIP responsive mode or use SENTRYPEER_SIP_RESPONSIVE env
arguments.raw: -r                           Enable SIP responsive mode or use SENTRYPEER_SIP_RESPONSIVE env

arguments.description: Disable SIP mode completely or use SENTRYPEER_SIP_DISABLE env
arguments.raw: -R                           Disable SIP mode completely or use SENTRYPEER_SIP_DISABLE env

arguments.description: Set JSON logfile (default './sentrypeer_json.log') location or use SENTRYPEER_JSON_LOG_FILE env
arguments.raw: -l <JSON_LOG_FILE>           Set JSON logfile (default './sentrypeer_json.log') location or use SENTRYPEER_JSON_LOG_FILE env

arguments.description: Disable Rust powered TCP, UDP and TLS or use SENTRYPEER_TLS_DISABLE env
arguments.raw: -N                           Disable Rust powered TCP, UDP and TLS or use SENTRYPEER_TLS_DISABLE env

arguments.description: Set TLS cert location (default './cert.pem') or use SENTRYPEER_CERT env
arguments.raw: -t <TLS_CERT_FILE>           Set TLS cert location (default './cert.pem') or use SENTRYPEER_CERT env

arguments.description: Set TLS key location (default './key.pem') or use SENTRYPEER_KEY env
arguments.raw: -k <TLS_KEY_FILE>            Set TLS key location (default './key.pem') or use SENTRYPEER_KEY env

arguments.description: Set TLS listen address (default '0.0.0.0:5061') or use SENTRYPEER_TLS_LISTEN_ADDRESS env
arguments.raw: -z <TLS_LISTEN_ADDRESS>      Set TLS listen address (default '0.0.0.0:5061') or use SENTRYPEER_TLS_LISTEN_ADDRESS env

arguments.description: Enable syslog logging or use SENTRYPEER_SYSLOG env
arguments.raw: -s                           Enable syslog logging or use SENTRYPEER_SYSLOG env

arguments.description: Enable verbose logging or use SENTRYPEER_VERBOSE env
arguments.raw: -v                           Enable verbose logging or use SENTRYPEER_VERBOSE env

arguments.description: Enable debug mode or use SENTRYPEER_DEBUG env
arguments.raw: -d                           Enable debug mode or use SENTRYPEER_DEBUG env

arguments.description: Print help
arguments.raw: -h, --help                   Print help

arguments.description: Print version
arguments.raw: -V, --version                Print version

commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# sentrypeer -h
 Protect your SIP Servers from bad actors at https://sentrypeer.com
 
 Usage: sentrypeer [OPTIONS]
 
 Options:
 -f <DB_FILE>                 Set 'sentrypeer.db' location or use SENTRYPEER_DB_FILE env
 -j                           Enable json logging or use SENTRYPEER_JSON_LOG env
 -p                           Enable Peer to Peer mode or use SENTRYPEER_PEER_TO_PEER env
 -b <BOOTSTRAP_NODE>          Set Peer to Peer bootstrap node or use SENTRYPEER_BOOTSTRAP_NODE env
 -i <CLIENT_ID>               Set OAuth 2 client ID or use SENTRYPEER_OAUTH2_CLIENT_ID env to get a Bearer token for WebHook
 -c <CLIENT_SECRET>           Set OAuth 2 client secret or use SENTRYPEER_OAUTH2_CLIENT_SECRET env to get a Bearer token for WebHook
 -a                           Enable RESTful API mode or use SENTRYPEER_API env
 -w <WEBHOOK_URL>             Set WebHook URL for bad actor json POSTs or use SENTRYPEER_WEBHOOK_URL env
 -r                           Enable SIP responsive mode or use SENTRYPEER_SIP_RESPONSIVE env
 -R                           Disable SIP mode completely or use SENTRYPEER_SIP_DISABLE env
 -l <JSON_LOG_FILE>           Set JSON logfile (default './sentrypeer_json.log') location or use SENTRYPEER_JSON_LOG_FILE env
 -N                           Disable Rust powered TCP, UDP and TLS or use SENTRYPEER_TLS_DISABLE env
 -t <TLS_CERT_FILE>           Set TLS cert location (default './cert.pem') or use SENTRYPEER_CERT env
 -k <TLS_KEY_FILE>            Set TLS key location (default './key.pem') or use SENTRYPEER_KEY env
 -z <TLS_LISTEN_ADDRESS>      Set TLS listen address (default '0.0.0.0:5061') or use SENTRYPEER_TLS_LISTEN_ADDRESS env
 -s                           Enable syslog logging or use SENTRYPEER_SYSLOG env
 -v                           Enable verbose logging or use SENTRYPEER_VERBOSE env
 -d                           Enable debug mode or use SENTRYPEER_DEBUG env
 -h, --help                   Print help
 -V, --version                Print version

local_help.man:
SentryPeer(1)               General Commands Manual               SentryPeer(1)

NAME
     sentrypeer - A distributed list of bad IP addresses and phone numbers col-
     lected via a SIP Honeypot

SYNOPSIS
     sentrypeer [-hVvjplfdrRaws]

DESCRIPTION
     SentryPeer is a fraud detection tool. It lets bad actors try to make phone
     calls  and  saves  the  IP address they came from and number they tried to
     call. Those details are then used to block them at the  service  providers
     network  and  the next time a user/customer tries to call a collected num-
     ber, it's blocked.

     Traditionally this data is shipped to a central place, so  you  don't  own
     the  data you've collected. This project is all about Peer to Peer sharing
     of that data. The user owning the data and various Service Provider / Net-
     work Provider related feeds of the data is the key bit for me. I'm sick of
     all the services out there that keep it and sell it.  If you've  collected
     it,  you  should have the choice to keep it and/or opt in to share it with
     other SentryPeer community members via p2p methods.

OPTIONS
     -f <DB_FILE>
            Set 'sentrypeer.db' location or use SENTRYPEER_DB_FILE env

     -j     Enable json logging or use SENTRYPEER_JSON_LOG env

     -p     Enable Peer to Peer mode or use SENTRYPEER_PEER_TO_PEER env

     -b <BOOTSTRAP_NODE>
            Set Peer to Peer bootstrap node  or  use  SENTRYPEER_BOOTSTRAP_NODE
            env

     -i <CLIENT_ID>
            Set OAuth 2 client ID or use SENTRYPEER_OAUTH2_CLIENT_ID env to get
            a Bearer token for WebHook

     -c <CLIENT_SECRET>
            Set  OAuth  2  client secret or use SENTRYPEER_OAUTH2_CLIENT_SECRET
            env to get a Bearer token for WebHook

     -a     Enable RESTful API mode or use SENTRYPEER_API env

     -w <WEBHOOK_URL>
            Set WebHook URL for bad actor json  POSTs  or  use  SENTRYPEER_WEB-
            HOOK_URL env

     -r     Enable SIP responsive mode or use SENTRYPEER_SIP_RESPONSIVE env

     -R     Disable SIP mode completely or use SENTRYPEER_SIP_DISABLE env

     -l <JSON_LOG_FILE>
            Set  JSON logfile (default './sentrypeer_json.log') location or use
            SENTRYPEER_JSON_LOG_FILE env

     -N     Disable Rust powered TCP, UDP and TLS or use SENTRYPEER_TLS_DISABLE
            env

     -t <TLS_CERT_FILE>
            Set TLS cert location (default './cert.pem') or use SENTRYPEER_CERT
            env

     -k <TLS_KEY_FILE>
            Set TLS key location (default './key.pem')  or  use  SENTRYPEER_KEY
            env

     -z <TLS_LISTEN_ADDRESS>
            Set  TLS  listen  address  (default  '0.0.0.0:5061')  or  use  SEN-
            TRYPEER_TLS_LISTEN_ADDRESS env

     -s     Enable syslog logging or use SENTRYPEER_SYSLOG env

     -v     Enable verbose logging or use SENTRYPEER_VERBOSE env

     -d     Enable debug mode or use SENTRYPEER_DEBUG env

     -h, --help
            Print help

     -V, --version
            Print version

USING SENTRYPEER
     You can run SentryPeer via the console, use a Docker image via Docker Hub,
     build your own container with the Dockerfile on GitHub or install a deb or
     rpm. It looks like you installed from source or a package if you're  read-
     ing this :-)

BUGS
     Issues and can be raised on the SentryPeer GitHub page:

     <https://github.com/ghenry/SentryPeer/issues>

     It's okay to raise an issue to ask a question.

SEE ALSO
     <https://sentrypeer.org>

LICENSE
     Copyright (c) 2021 - 2025 Gavin Henry <ghenry@sentrypeer.org>
     License   GPLv2   or   GPLv3:   GNU   GPL   version   2   or   version   3
     <https://www.gnu.org/licenses/gpl.html> This is  free  software;  you  are
     free  to  change and redistribute it.  There is NO WARRANTY, to the extent
     permitted by law.

AUTHORS/CREDITS
     SentryPeer was written by Gavin Henry <ghenry@sentrypeer.org>

SentryPeer                       December 2024                    SentryPeer(1)

apt_metadata.depends: adduser, libc6 (>= 2.38), libcurl4t64 (>= 7.16.2), libgcc-s1 (>= 4.2), libjansson4 (>= 2.14), libmicrohttpd12t64 (>= 0.9.50), libosip2-15t64 (>= 5.3.0), libpcre2-8-0 (>= 10.22), libsqlite3-0 (>= 3.5.9), libuuid1 (>= 2.16)
apt_metadata.description:
SIP peer to peer honeypot for VoIP
SentryPeer is a distributed list of bad IP addresses and phone numbers
collected via a SIP Honeypot.
SentryPeer is a fraud detection tool. It lets bad actors try to make phone
calls and saves the IP address they came from and number they tried to call.
Those details can then be used to raise notifications at the service providers
network and the next time a user/customer tries to call a collected number,
you can act anyway you see fit.
.
Traditionally this data is shipped to a central place, so you don't own
the data you've collected. This project is all about Peer to Peer sharing
of that data. The user owning the data and various Service Provider /
Network Provider related feeds of the data is the key bit for me. I'm
sick of all the services out there that keep it and sell it. If you've
collected it, you should have the choice to keep it and/or opt in to
share it with other SentryPeer community members via p2p methods.
